mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 1.0% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings. This mechanism operates continuously and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when FEDFUNDS is in the sub-1% regime.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not statistically significantly higher than when FEDFUNDS >= 1.0% (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 9.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions cascade through equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE > 9.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% month-over-month while simultaneously unemployment rises, indicating weakening consumer demand that compresses profit margins and equity valuations. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of instances, or the joint signal has lower predictive power (Rank IC) than either component alone over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) index declines month-over-month below 100.0, signaling supply-side contraction that reduces corporate output capacity and earnings growth. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as supply constraints become visible to markets.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances when INDPRO < 100.0 and declining, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) exceed 600 thousand units annualized while real GDP growth (GDPC1) is positive, indicating robust demand-side expansion that supports corporate earnings through construction, materials, and consumer wealth effects. This interaction predicts positive returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 45% of instances, or the interaction signal has lower Rank IC than either component alone over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 50 thousand new jobs per month, signaling labor market weakness that precedes earnings downgrades and equity multiple compression. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when PAYEMS growth < 50 thousand, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 220.0 index points while federal funds rate remains below 0.5%, creating a negative real rate environment that erodes equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as rate expectations shift.
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of instances, or the interaction signal exhibits lower Rank IC than either component alone over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth exceeds 100.5 index points while payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows above 100 thousand per month, indicating synchronized supply and labor expansion that supports sustained earnings growth and positive equity returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 45% of instances, or the interaction signal has lower Rank IC than either component alone over rolling 3-year windows.
